message:
refactor(palette): extract layout helpers, centralize viewport sync, gate animations

Major refactor of the palette command list rendering pipeline to
eliminate duplicated layout math and fix viewport sync inconsistencies:

Layout extraction:
- commandPhaseLayout() consolidates the split-view width calculation
  (min/max column clamping, tier-aware split proportions) that was
  previously inlined in both viewCommandPhase() and the WindowSizeMsg
  handler. Single source of truth for list/preview column widths.
- commandListBoxHeight() extracts the height-14/min-5 calculation.

Viewport sync centralization:
- New syncListViewport() method is the single call site that sets
  viewport Width, Height, calls SetContent with rendered command list,
  and ensures cursor visibility. Previously these were split across
  the WindowSizeMsg handler (which set dimensions) and viewCommandPhase()
  (which called SetContent), leading to stale viewport content when
  the model mutated between Update and View.
- syncListViewport() is now called from: NewWithOptions (initial build),
  WindowSizeMsg, AnimationTickMsg, recentsMsg, favorite/pin toggle,
  filter update, and the default key handler fallthrough.
- ensureCursorVisible() now uses SetYOffset() instead of raw YOffset
  assignment, which was a workaround for nil-lines in a value-receiver
  View(). With syncListViewport() always populating content before
  cursor adjustment, the workaround is no longer needed.

Animation gating:
- Model gains an animate bool field, initialized from
  styles.AnimationsEnabled(). Init() only schedules the tick command
  when animate is true. AnimationTickMsg handler short-circuits when
  animate is false, preventing CPU-wasting tick chains inside tmux.
- SessionSelector mirrors the same pattern: new animate field, guarded
  Init() and Update(AnimationTickMsg).

Tests:
- TestNew_DisablesAnimationInTmuxByDefault verifies the palette model
  does not schedule or advance ticks inside tmux.
- Existing TestUpdateAnimationTick and TestSessionSelectorInit now set
  the full environment matrix to ensure animation ticks fire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
